data.narrative:
QuEra Computing is a Series B quantum computing company that has raised $230M to date. The valuation universe comprises 11 peers across quantum computing: 3 publicly traded firms (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private companies including Q-CTRL, Quantinuum, and others at varying stages.

Peer-median analysis proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables in the quantum sector—only one direct peer at Series B with published valuation data, falling short of the three-comparable threshold required for reliable cross-sectional analysis. Stage-progression methodology is the applicable approach here. Using QuEra's $230M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B-to-later progression, this method yields $437M.

The quantitative analysis supports a valuation range of $371M to $503M, with a median point of $437M derived from the stage-progression method. Key uncertainties include the absence of sufficient private-market comparables at identical stage and tier, limiting the ability to triangulate via peer multiples. Given quantum computing's heterogeneous technology approaches and business models, reliance on a single method introduces model risk. Resolution of this uncertainty would require either additional same-stage public disclosures or a broader set of private financing rounds with disclosed valuations in the quantum sector.
